boundary: Mappings certify only the stated formal kernel. Conditional and partial rows do not promote the full prose statement to a Lean theorem.

entries.note: Explicit two-strategy replicator contraction: each step retains a fixed fraction q in [0,1) of the remaining non-MAP population, yielding geometric convergence of MAP share to one. A constant one-half population under strictly increasing drift proves that drift growth alone does not supply the persistent fitness/contraction bridge.

Strategy-space balance kernel: positive cooperation converts strict viability into reflection capacity above drift divided by cooperation; isolated strategies cannot balance nonnegative drift. Existence follows from an explicit available-strategy witness. Sub-maximal drift alone does not populate the strategy inventory, and exact cancellation has no strict viability margin.

Variational sign kernel: reflection production exceeding thermal entropy cost is exactly positive free-energy rate, and the reverse inequality is exactly negative rate. The printed MAP/MAD limit claims require additional boundedness or dissipation laws; weak-coupling decoupling consumes an explicit vanishing-interaction hypothesis.

Stateful two-threshold kernel: lower < 1 < upper implies an ordered hysteresis band; crossing below switches MAP out, crossing above switches into MAP, and either regime persists inside the band. Thus the same in-band coupling has different outcomes by incoming history. A memoryless classifier cannot express this behavior.

For the exact exponential step law, positive MAP energy strictly grows above Lambda=1, positive MAD energy remains positive but strictly decays beyond |Lambda|=1, and the MAP update is identity at the boundary. A countermodel separates boundary crossing from the load-bearing evolution law.

Strong coupling is classified by polarity, weak coupling decouples, equality remains an explicit critical regime, and polarity reversal exchanges MAP/MAD. A countermodel shows these parameter conditions do not themselves entail the source's free-energy limits without a dynamics bridge.

The displayed residual hypotheses yield a linear rho bound by the triangle inequality. A concrete countermodel refutes the stated rho-squared conclusion; that conclusion is recovered when both residuals are assumed quadratic-order.

Deterministic scalar kernel: positive external reflection strictly raises sustainable drift and creates an interval where MAP has positive viability margin after isolation does not. The paper's probability-limit language remains conditional because no probability law is specified.

In the scalar worst-case model, positive contribution is exactly restorative strength exceeding the combined drift burden. A countermodel shows the proposition's stated positive-polarity and kappa-critical conditions alone are insufficient without that drift-relative margin.

Adaptation is explicit structure selecting transfer, reflection, or drift modulation that preserves viability across a condition change. A countermodel proves that merely having the three operators does not manufacture the adaptation law.

Covenant tuple as data with sign-carrying stability; the MAD dual is an involution that flips stability but not coupling strength.

Exact finite-positive-p and Linfinity formulas, special values, monotonicity in positive finite p, and divergence of Linfinity torsion along unbounded support.
The logarithmic diagnostic is 2-1/p for finite p on support at least two, with the support-one convention and p=1,2,infinity values certified.
Finite admissible-path kernel with symbolic candidates explicitly included in geometric candidates; proves the exact minimum and representability criteria.
For finite path families, decoherence is the nonnegative excess symbolic minimum, vanishing exactly when a symbolic geodesic exists; the diagonal gap is exact.
Phi is the unique positive balanced-recursion root in the memory coordinate and is proved distinct from the Euclidean norm ratio.
Balanced memory ratios converge to the unique phi coordinate, while sqrt(2) remains a distinct metric-fracture coordinate.

Given the stated observer-resolved linear equivalence and diagonal evolution law, four-channel reconstruction, uniqueness, and invariance are proved. Existence of such a decomposition is not asserted universally.
Drift/reflection stabilization cancels only its budget channel. Zero energy derivative additionally requires external closure; a compiled countermodel refutes conservation from balance alone.
Represented as explicit MetabolicPersistenceLaw data, not a Lean axiom or a definitional implication.

Stationary iff critical point of F_proc for the descent step; the minimizer/critical gap stays open.
SRMF convergence to a fixed point (contraction-Banach); the Wasserstein O(1/t) rate stays open.
The recursive reflective flow converges to a unique stable fixed point.
Operators evolve to and rest at the unique viable fixed point.
Metabolic capacity non-decreasing in energy reserves.
Viability antitone in spectral radius: minimizing rho maximizes viability.
Finite discrete strategy-set kernel: eventual two-sided ESS-to-MAP identification yields distance convergence to zero; population concentration alone does not imply equality of the abstract sets.

Finite optimization kernel: a nonempty operator inventory has a process-free-energy minimizer, whose certificate dominates every incumbent and rejects strictly suboptimal choices. Viability alone does not imply that selection law; SRMF dynamics remain an explicit bridge.

Explicit adaptation-law kernel: below-threshold effectiveness creates deficit-proportional refinement pressure under a positive feedback gain; a stable gradient step descends quadratic process free energy; and execution cost may rise while total process energy falls. The threshold inequality alone does not force an update.

Composition kernel: bounded marginal fidelity per reflective level plus a logarithmic metabolic recursion-depth bound yields the printed logarithmic accuracy envelope. Metabolic capacity alone cannot constrain an otherwise unrestricted fidelity value.

Exact radius preservation transports shade, but radial-order preservation alone does not. Golden spiral steps add log(phi) to log-radius; bounded normalized shade is not multiplicative. Balanced reciprocity has rate phi, while zero reciprocal weight has unit radial rate.
